items.summary: The ongoing DTW Ignite 2026 conference [1.] represents a major shift for the telecom industry from theoretical AI experimentation to practical, code-proven execution under the core theme, “The Future Faster.” “The Race to 2030” & Core Theme: Shift to Action: TM Forum Vice President Aaron Boasman-Patel and CEO Nik...

items.title: SNS Telecom & IT: Private 5G Market to Reach $6.6 Billion as Physical AI Takes Hold
items.summary: Private 5G networks are transitioning from limited-scale deployments to a more material segment of the wireless infrastructure market. SNS Telecom & IT estimates that annual spending on private 5G networks will exceed $6.6 billion by 2029, driven by multi-site and multi-national enterprise rollouts supporting...

items.title: AI-Era Cloud Network Transformation: A Reference Architecture and Implementation Roadmap
items.summary: By Shazia Hasnie, PhD Introduction: The physical network infrastructure that underpins cloud computing was designed for an era that no longer exists. Distributed training across hundreds of thousands of GPUs, real-time inference at the edge, and autonomous agent coordination impose requirements that traditional cloud...

items.title: AI Is Designing Radio Chips That Humans Couldn’t Even Imagine
items.summary: Summary RFIC design is a complex “ dark art ” that limits progress in wireless technologies like 5G, autonomous vehicles, and satellite communications. Princeton researchers use reinforcement learning and inverse design to rapidly create RFICs from scratch. Diffusion models rapidly generate novel or...

items.title: Home Broadband Is 5G’s Surprise Killer App
items.summary: 5G telecommunications, according to industry hype when 5G first launched in 2019 , was going to be all about buzzy applications like mobile augmented reality and autonomous vehicles . But the surprise plot twist came when replacing home cable internet turned into 5G’s most widely adopted new application. Fixed...

items.title: The Hidden 6G Bottleneck: RF Hardware Design Is Becoming a Strategic Race
items.summary: As 5G-Advanced matures and 6G research moves closer to implementation, the wireless industry faces a deeper challenge than spectrum, standards or AI-native network architecture. Future wireless systems will depend on whether the industry can design, validate and manufacture increasingly complex RF modules fast enough.

items.title: 6G in Dalian: What the Latest 3GPP Meetings Reveal About the Future Radio and Network
items.summary: The 6G physical layer is starting to converge. The protocol stack is being simplified in meaningful places. But the most consequential architecture decisions are now moving toward the June plenary in Singapore.

items.title: one6G Supports IEEE Communications Standards Magazine Special Issue on empowering robotics with 6G
items.summary: one6G is pleased to highlight the IEEE Communications Standards Magazine special issue, “Empowering Robotics With 6G: Connectivity, Intelligence, and Beyond.” Guest-edited by Mona Ghassemian, Kaspar Althoefer, Aryan Kaushik, Mahdi Tavakoli, Douwe Dresscher, and Xueli An, this publication […] The post one6G Supports...

items.title: Satellite IoT Contenders Are Racing Against a 6G Deadline
items.summary: The decades-old “ satellite-IoT” race , in which Internet of Things devices connect to satellites in orbit, has reached an inflection point in 2026. Satellite-IoT is growing in response to a rising need for connected devices in applications including livestock tracking , wildfire detection , global supply-chain...

items.title: Direct-to-Cell Technology: Enabling Satellite Connectivity for Legacy Devices
items.summary: Direct-to-cell technology uses LEO satellites as spaceborne cell towers. It delivers LTE services to existing smartphones without hardware changes, bridging global coverage gaps. What Attendees will Learn How DTC works as a spaceborne cell tower — LEO satellites carry LTE eNodeB payloads in regenerative mode. How they...

items.title: Starlink and Amazon Could Gain Access to Europe’s Satellite Airwaves
items.summary: The European Union plans to reserve most future satellite spectrum for regional firms while still allowing Starlink and Amazon to compete, reflecting Brussels' attempt to balance technological sovereignty with market openness in a strategically sensitive communications sector.

items.title: Ericsson is building the future of 6G, AI and drone detection
items.summary: At its Americas' HQ in Plano, Texas, Ericsson completed the world's first pre-standard 6G session and a live drone-detection trial using existing network infrastructure.

items.title: Orange Belgium picks Nokia for optical network upgrade
items.summary: Nokia is the sole supplier for a major transformation of the operator’s transport infrastructure, unifying fixed and mobile networks into a single, converged optical transport network across Belgium.

items.title: Is SpaceX developing a mobile phone now too?
items.summary: Between a new report about a possible Starlink AI device and ongoing speculation about Elon Musk’s terrestrial network aspirations, SpaceX is shaking up the wireless industry like nobody’s business.

items.title: Inseego boosts its FWA team with 2 ex-Nokia execs
items.summary: Inseego pulled in two ex-Nokia execs for its FWA team and expanded the CCO's coverage to lead the Americas, including Latin America.

items.title: FCC tees up big auction for C-band ‘super band’
items.summary: The industry just finished a little AWS-3 auction and now it’s gearing up for a much bigger one: 160 megahertz in the upper C-band next year.

items.title: FCC scheme would approve satellite licenses in assembly-line process
items.summary: The goal of the Space Modernization Order is to turn the approval of applications, for both satellites and earth stations, into an “assembly line process,” according to FCC Chairman Brendan Carr.

items.title: Rakuten Symphony taps Amit Bhardwaj as Head of RAN Business Unit
items.summary: Bhardwaj will help drive the continued evolution and deployment of cloud-native, open RAN solutions for operators worldwide.

items.title: Korean operators ramp up AI investments to make country an AI superpower
items.summary: Korean telcos are racing to turn AI ambition into infrastructure reality, with SK Telecom, KT and LG Uplus pouring billions into data centers, compute and connectivity as South Korea pushes to become a top-three global AI power.

items.title: Opinion: Despite obstacles, mobile and cloud are destined for a meet-cute
items.summary: Mobile network software is quickly becoming a stand-alone business that is not tied to hardware.

items.title: T-Mobile to shut down 2G GSM network this summer
items.summary: It’s hard to believe T-Mobile still operates a 2G GSM network, but it’s true – although not for long.

items.title: Telcos want autonomous networks, but Ericsson says trust remains key hurdle
items.summary: Ericsson says telcos can’t automate their way into true network autonomy until they solve the harder problem: trusting AI to run the network without humans in the loop.

items.title: Will there be an AI cost reckoning for telcos? Here’s what AWS’ telco chief thinks.
items.summary: Telcos are eager to unleash AI on their networks, but AWS’ Jan Hofmeyr warns they could face a costly wake-up call if they mistake generic LLMs for a network-ready fix.
